100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 1, 1926, the International Workers of the World announced a significant strike in France, escalating tensions between labor and government amid rising discontent over economic conditions. In the United States, the Senate continued debates over the Veterans’ Bonus Bill, with veterans pressing for immediate payment. Meanwhile, in Egypt, political unrest surged as protests against British influence intensified, signaling a growing demand for independence.
Updated: Monday, June 1, 2026 at 12:06:25 PM CDT
